statement.markdown: Given an array of integers representing a max heap, remove the maximum element and recover the max heap. Your function should take in a list of integers representing the values in the heap and return the list with the max element removed and the heap recovered.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a list of integers representing a max heap.
statement.outputFormat: Output is a list of integers representing the max heap after the maximum element has been removed.
statement.notes: The size of the input list will be between 1 and 10,000. The input list will only contain non-negative integers.

examples.explanation: The maximum element in the heap is 3. After removing it and recovering the heap, the new heap is [2, 3, 0, 5, 3, 3].

hints.text: Remember to use the swap operation to restore the heap property after removing the maximum element.

editorial.title: Recover Max Heap After Removal Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to first remove the maximum element from the heap, then to restore the heap property by swapping elements until the heap is valid again.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to heapify the array after removing the maximum element. This would involve heapifying the array from the root node, then moving down the tree, heapifying each subtree until the entire heap is valid again.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to first remove the maximum element, then to restore the heap property by swapping elements until the heap is valid again. We can use a combination of a max heap and a binary heap to achieve this in O(n log n) time.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of this approach can be proven by induction on the size of the heap. The base case is when the heap size is 0, in which case the heap is already valid. The inductive step is to show that if the heap is valid for size n, then it remains valid after removing an element and restoring the heap property.
editorial.dryRun: Step 1: Remove the maximum element from the heap. Step 2: Restore the heap property by swapping elements until the heap is valid again.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to swap elements to restore the heap property.
Using the wrong data structure for the heap.
